data.title: KeyCorp announces board changes and stronger quarterly results
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

KeyCorp ( (KEY) ) has provided an announcement.

On January 15, 2026, KeyCorp directors Ruth Ann Gillis and Carlton Highsmith notified the board that they will retire at the end of their terms at the 2026 annual shareholder meeting, prompting the board’s January 20, 2026 decision to nominate former BlackRock executive Antonio “Tony” DeSpirito and former Truist executive Christopher L. “Chris” Henson as new independent directors, while appointing Todd Vasos as lead independent director to succeed Alexander M. “Sandy” Cutler. These board changes are intended to support the next phase of value creation, adding deep asset management and banking experience while keeping the board at 14 members. Also on January 20, 2026, KeyCorp reported fourth-quarter 2025 net income from continuing operations attributable to common shareholders of $474 million, or $0.43 per diluted share, up from $454 million in the prior quarter and reversing a loss in the year-ago period, driven by record full-year revenue of $7.5 billion, higher net interest income and margin, improved credit quality, and robust fee businesses, including record assets under management of $70 billion and near-record investment banking and debt placement fees. The bank, which repurchased $200 million of common shares in the quarter while maintaining a Common Equity Tier 1 ratio of 11.7%, highlighted strong operating leverage, substantial growth in tangible book value, and sustained momentum as it targets mid-to-high teens returns on tangible common equity and signals capacity for increased capital returns to shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (KEY) stock is a Hold with a $24.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KeyCorp stock, see the KEY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KEY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KEY is a Neutral.

KeyCorp’s overall stock score of 69 reflects a strong recovery in financial performance, with improved revenue growth and profitability. The earnings call highlighted strategic growth and a robust capital position, although challenges in expense management and consumer loan growth remain. Technical analysis suggests bullish momentum, but the stock appears overbought. Valuation concerns persist due to a high P/E ratio, though the dividend yield offers some compensation.

To see Spark’s full report on KEY stock, click here.

More about KeyCorp

KeyCorp, headquartered in Cleveland and listed on the NYSE under the symbol KEY, operates as a U.S. regional banking institution offering a broad range of financial services, including commercial and consumer banking, wealth management, and investment banking. The company focuses on generating organic growth through strategic investments in frontline bankers and technology, maintaining strong capital ratios while returning capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

Average Trading Volume: 16,210,151

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $22.99B

data.title: Fifth Third Bancorp posts strong Q4 2025 results
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fifth Third Bancorp ( (FITB) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Fifth Third Bancorp reported fourth-quarter 2025 diluted earnings per share of $1.04, as strong business momentum and improved credit trends drove a 1.36% return on average assets and a 14.0% return on average common equity. Net income available to common shareholders rose to $699 million, supported by record annual net interest income of $6 billion, 5% year-over-year loan growth (including 7% growth in middle-market loans), 4% annual demand deposit growth, and a 16% increase in assets under management to $80 billion. Credit quality improved, with net charge-offs declining to 40 basis points and nonperforming assets at 0.65%, while capital strength increased, with the CET1 ratio rising to 10.77% and tangible book value per share up 21% from a year earlier. The bank also generated 230 basis points of positive operating leverage in 2025, maintained an adjusted efficiency ratio near mid-50s, and returned $1.6 billion of capital to shareholders, underscoring its solid profitability and reinforcing its competitive positioning among regional lenders.

The most recent analyst rating on (FITB) stock is a Buy with a $53.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fifth Third Bancorp stock, see the FITB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FITB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FITB is a Outperform.

The score is driven most by solid underlying financial profitability/leverage, supported by favorable valuation (reasonable P/E and a ~3.2% yield). Technicals are constructive but not strongly overextended, while earnings-call positives (revenue/NII growth, operating leverage, improving credit metrics, capital returns) are partially offset by the Tricolor fraud provisioning and portfolio risk considerations.

To see Spark’s full report on FITB stock, click here.

More about Fifth Third Bancorp

Fifth Third Bancorp is a U.S. regional financial services company operating in commercial and consumer banking, wealth management and related financial services. The bank focuses on loan and deposit growth across its footprint, with a particular emphasis on expanding in high‑growth Southeast markets and increasing consumer household relationships and assets under management.

data.title: Ecopetrol Sets Governance Measures for February 5, 2026 Extraordinary Shareholders’ Meeting
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Ecopetrol SA ( (EC) ) is now available.

On January 19, 2026, Ecopetrol S.A. announced a series of governance and procedural measures to ensure proper shareholder representation at its Extraordinary Shareholders’ Meeting scheduled for February 5, 2026. The company will formally remind investors of their right to be represented by proxy and detail the legal requirements for valid powers of attorney, empower its Corporate Legal Vice Presidency and General Secretariat to review and verify these proxies, and instruct meeting personnel to reject any that do not meet legal standards or fail to clearly identify the designated representative. Ecopetrol is also reinforcing restrictions on the conduct of managers and employees, prohibiting them from influencing shareholders’ choice of proxies, from recommending voting slates or coordinating voting strategies, from acting as attorneys-in-fact for others’ shares except in limited legal cases, and from receiving proxy appointments if they are directly or indirectly connected to company management. These steps underscore the company’s effort to align with Colombian regulatory guidance, bolster corporate governance practices, and provide greater transparency and safeguards for minority shareholders ahead of a key extraordinary meeting.

The most recent analyst rating on (EC) stock is a Buy with a $13.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ecopetrol SA stock, see the EC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on EC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, EC is a Outperform.

Ecopetrol’s overall stock score is driven by strong technical indicators and attractive valuation, despite financial challenges and high leverage. The positive sentiment from the earnings call further supports the stock’s potential, although caution is advised due to overbought technical signals.

To see Spark’s full report on EC stock, click here.

More about Ecopetrol SA

Ecopetrol S.A. is Colombia’s largest company and one of the main integrated energy groups in the Americas, employing more than 19,000 people. It accounts for over 60% of Colombia’s hydrocarbon production and operates key transportation, logistics and refining systems, while holding leading positions in petrochemicals and gas distribution. Following the acquisition of a majority stake in ISA, Ecopetrol also participates in energy transmission, real-time system management and road concessions in Colombia and across Brazil, Chile, Peru and Bolivia, as well as in telecommunications, and it maintains upstream exploration and production interests in the United States, Brazil and Mexico.

Average Trading Volume: 2,372,341

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $25.33B

The latest update is out from Avadel Pharmaceuticals ( (AVDL) ).

On January 19, 2026, Avadel Pharmaceuticals announced that the High Court of Ireland has scheduled a sanction hearing for February 10, 2026 to consider a scheme of arrangement under which Alkermes plc will acquire all issued and to-be-issued ordinary shares of Avadel. The offer period for this takeover began on October 22, 2025, and the transaction is expected to close shortly after the court sanctions the scheme, with the company outlining Irish Takeover Rules disclosure obligations for shareholders holding or dealing in 1% or more of Avadel’s relevant securities, underscoring the formal progression and regulatory framework of the pending acquisition.

ING Groep ( (ING) ) has provided an announcement.

On 20 January 2026, ING Groep reported weekly progress on its €1.1 billion share buyback programme launched on 30 October 2025, confirming that it repurchased 1,822,110 shares between 12 and 16 January 2026 at an average price of €24.87, for a total of about €45.3 million. In line with the programme’s aim of reducing ING’s share capital, the bank noted that it has cumulatively bought back 21,006,882 shares at an average price of €22.94, representing roughly €481.9 million in consideration and completion of about 43.81% of the planned buyback, underscoring an ongoing commitment to capital return and balance-sheet optimisation for shareholders.

data.title: Delta Air Lines Insider Shake-Up: Top Brass Quietly Cashes In on Major Stock Sales
data.description:
New insider activity at Delta Air Lines ( (DAL) ) has taken place on January 16, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Several top executives at Delta Air Lines have recently unloaded significant amounts of company stock, with combined sales totaling well over $20 million. President Glen Hauenstein sold 20,000 shares for $1,400,000, while CEO Edward Bastian executed the largest single transaction, selling 173,230 shares valued at $12,299,330. Chief External Affairs Officer Peter Carter sold 14,300 shares for $1,005,290, and EVP – Global Sales & Distrib Steven M. Sear disposed of 31,290 shares for $2,180,287. In the technology leadership ranks, EVP & Chief Information Officer Rahul D. Samant sold 57,003 shares worth $3,965,698. Rounding out the wave of insider selling, EVP & President – International Alain Bellemare sold 36,000 shares for $2,491,560. These coordinated insider moves highlight a notable period of stock selling among Delta’s senior leadership team.

Recent Updates on DAL stock

Over the last 24 hours, Delta Air Lines has seen several analyst updates tied closely to its recent earnings call and 2026 outlook. Seaport Research trimmed its target mainly due to broader macroeconomic volatility, suggesting that market-wide uncertainty, rather than company-specific weakness, warranted a slightly more cautious stance. UBS also modestly lowered its target while emphasizing that Delta’s guidance for 2026 is deliberately conservative, leaving room for upside if current demand trends and execution hold. In contrast, Argus raised its target after Delta’s strong Q4 results, citing the company’s increasingly diversified and higher‑margin revenue mix—especially premium, loyalty, cargo, and maintenance services—and expectations that business and premium travel demand will remain solid into 2026. Across the commentary, analysts are reacting to Delta’s record revenue and free cash flow, strong premium and co‑brand credit card performance, and clearer long‑term growth plan, while balancing these positives against macro volatility, regulatory risk, and some lingering cost and operational challenges. Recent management commentary also emphasized robust current demand, including record cash sales and expectations for mid‑single‑digit revenue growth in the March quarter, reinforcing the view that Delta is in a durable earnings upcycle even as analysts fine‑tune their models and targets around these factors.

Spark’s Take on DAL Stock

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (profit and cash flow recovery with improved leverage) and a constructive earnings outlook (2026 EPS/FCF guidance and continued deleveraging). data.title: Top Brass Doubles Down on Acrivon With Bold Insider Buying Spree
data.description:
New insider activity at Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. ( (ACRV) ) has taken place on January 16, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. has seen notable insider buying activity, signaling confidence from top leadership. President & CEO Peter Blume-Jensen purchased 49,000 shares of Acrivon Therapeutics stock in a transaction valued at $81,830. Adding to this insider accumulation, CFO Adam D. Levy acquired 8,832 shares of the company’s stock, representing an additional investment of $14,926. These combined purchases underscore meaningful personal financial commitments by both the CEO and CFO to Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc.

Recent Updates on ACRV stock

In the last 24 hours, Acrivon Therapeutics (ACRV) has seen its outlook influenced mainly by a broad clinical and pipeline update and a follow-on R&D event, which together prompted at least one analyst to lift their price target. The key driver is stronger-than-expected Phase 2b data for lead asset ACR-368 in endometrial cancer, where updated interim results showed a 39% overall response rate in biomarker-positive monotherapy patients, higher response rates (44%) in less heavily pretreated patients, and particularly robust activity in serous endometrial cancer, including confirmed responses above 50% in certain subgroups. These data support a registrational-intent path, justify the strategic refocus and expansion of the serous endometrial cancer arm across European sites, and underpin plans for a Phase 3 confirmatory trial combining ACR-368 with anti–PD-1 therapy, all of which improve perceived probability of clinical and regulatory success. Additional upside to sentiment comes from early but favorable Phase 1 data for ACR-2316, which showed acceptable tolerability and tumor shrinkage (including partial responses in multiple AP3-prioritized tumor types), and from the nomination of ACR-6840, a potential first-in-class CDK11 inhibitor with an IND targeted for late 2026, reinforcing the depth and sustainability of the AP3-driven precision oncology pipeline. While technical indicators are currently constructive and the company maintains a low-debt balance sheet, some analysts remain cautious due to the absence of revenue, ongoing cash burn, and long-dated clinical risk, tempering enthusiasm despite the positive trial updates and contributing to a more balanced overall rating profile.

Spark’s Take on ACRV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ACRV is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance—no revenue, large and persistent losses, and substantial cash burn—despite low leverage. Technicals remain soft with the stock below key short- and mid-term moving averages, while valuation support is limited due to losses and no dividend. Offsetting these risks, recent positive clinical data and pipeline progress modestly improve the outlook.

data.description:

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Executive Vice President of Human Resources Michael Theilmann has executed a notable stock transaction involving Albertsons Companies, selling 49,000 shares. The sale, valued at $863,673, highlights a significant move by a key member of the company’s leadership team and may draw attention from investors monitoring insider trading activity and executive confidence in the company’s future performance.

Recent Updates on ACI stock

Over the last day, Albertsons has seen a flurry of analyst actions driven largely by a combination of competitive and macro headwinds offset by solid recent execution. Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock, citing ongoing market share losses, pressure in the pharmacy business, and a disinflationary backdrop that is intensifying “grocery wars,” with rising price competition, e-commerce partnerships, and promotional activity expected to squeeze margins. BofA trimmed its expectations after Q3 results, noting that while sales and earnings were supported by strong pharmacy and digital growth, the firm brought longer-term earnings forecasts in line with the company’s more modest updated guidance. Telsey Advisory also reduced its target despite better-than-expected Q3 results, acknowledging robust digital and pharmacy momentum and continued progress in strategic initiatives such as digital engagement, retail media, customer value enhancement, and productivity, but implicitly reflecting a more constrained outlook. Deutsche Bank’s resumed coverage framed 2026 as a mixed year for food retail, highlighting food disinflation, reduced government benefits, and consumers’ heightened value focus as challenges, even as potential stimulus later in 2026 could provide tailwinds. UBS lowered its target as well, without elaborating in detail, consistent with the broader theme that, despite solid company-specific execution, sector-wide margin and demand headwinds are tempering analysts’ medium-term expectations. Price action over the past 24 hours was influenced by this cluster of cautious revisions, as the market digested both the strong recent performance in digital and pharmacy and the tougher competitive and macro setup ahead.

Spark’s Take on ACI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ACI is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by high leverage and weakening margins, plus bearish technicals with the stock trending below key moving averages. These are partially offset by supportive valuation (low P/E and ~3.7% yield) and a constructive earnings-call backdrop with narrowed guidance, strong digital/pharmacy execution, and continued capital return plans, albeit with notable near-term headwinds.

Significant Clene shareholder Chidozie Ugwumba recently sold 58,289 shares of Clene stock in a transaction valued at $292,184, signaling a notable change in his investment position with the company.

Recent Updates on CLNN stock

In the last 24 hours, news on Clene has centered on two key developments: financing and regulatory progress for its ALS candidate CNM-Au8, both of which underpin recent analyst reassessments of the stock. On the financial side, Clene entered into a multi‑tranche registered direct offering of over $28 million, with an initial ~$6 million equity raise at $6.50 per unit already closed and additional capital contingent on the exercise of Series A and B warrants. This structure is explicitly tied to future FDA milestones and share‑price performance, and is expected to extend the company’s cash runway into at least the third quarter of 2026, with warrant proceeds potentially funding operations and commercialization efforts through late 2026 or early 2027—addressing going‑concern and cash‑burn risks that had weighed on sentiment. On the regulatory and clinical front, the FDA granted Clene an in‑person Type C meeting in Q1 2026 to review biomarker data supporting a potential NDA for CNM‑Au8 in ALS; Clene’s briefing package highlights statistically significant reductions in NfL and GFAP, along with independent cohort analyses linking modest NfL reductions to meaningfully lower mortality risk, and exploratory HEALEY Platform Trial data suggesting improved survival and a large mortality risk reduction among patients with IGFBP7 declines. These biomarker and survival findings have improved the perceived probability of regulatory success and enhanced the drug’s commercial potential, which is why they feature prominently in the rationale behind analyst price‑target revisions, even as overall ratings remain constrained by Clene’s weak financial profile, ongoing losses, and bearish technical signals.
